entries.title: Mahito's Idle Transfiguration Demands Soul-Level Guard

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen Shibuya Incident manga material through immediate aftermath.

content.fact: Mahito's Idle Transfiguration remains a soul-level threat in Shibuya, making contact and status outcomes unusually dangerous.
content.injection: During Mahito's Shibuya phase, treat Idle Transfiguration as a direct soul-level danger, especially for Yuji, Nanami, and Nobara scenes.
constraints: Do not soften Idle Transfiguration into ordinary physical injury.
antiLore: Do not let characters shrug off Mahito contact as routine battle damage unless the scene has a canon-supported exception.

sourceInfo.title: Shibuya Incident
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Nanami And Nobara Statuses Are Not Safe To Assume

context.label: Nanami and Nobara Shibuya status guard

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen Shibuya Incident manga material through immediate aftermath.

content.fact: Late Shibuya leaves Nanami and Nobara in major spoiler-bearing status states that should not be assumed in earlier Contexts.
content.injection: When late Shibuya status is active, handle Nanami and Nobara with explicit spoiler care; before that point, do not foreshadow or assume their late-incident outcomes.
constraints: Use later decks for post-Shibuya follow-through on Nobara's status.
antiLore: Do not casually mention Nanami or Nobara late-Shibuya outcomes in pre-Shibuya or early-Shibuya scenes.

sourceInfo.title: Shibuya Incident
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Kenjaku Reveal Reframes Geto Body Context

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen Shibuya Incident manga material through immediate aftermath.

content.fact: The Shibuya aftermath reveal reframes the apparent Geto body context around Kenjaku rather than treating Suguru Geto's original identity as straightforwardly active.
content.injection: At and after the Shibuya aftermath reveal, distinguish Kenjaku's control of Geto's body from Suguru Geto's earlier life and Jujutsu Kaisen 0 state.
constraints: Do not reveal this identity gate before Shibuya aftermath.
Use later decks for Culling Game execution of Kenjaku's broader plan.
antiLore: Do not call post-reveal Shibuya's body-controller state simply Geto acting with no distinction.

sourceInfo.title: Shibuya Incident
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Shibuya Aftermath Breaks The Pre-Incident Status Quo

continuity.sourceBoundary: Jujutsu Kaisen Shibuya Incident manga material through immediate aftermath.

content.fact: The Shibuya aftermath breaks the pre-incident status quo: Gojo is unavailable, the city has suffered mass harm, and jujutsu society enters a crisis state.
content.injection: At post-Shibuya Context, treat the world as politically and emotionally destabilized rather than returning to the pre-incident school-mission rhythm.
constraints: Use the Post-Shibuya and Perfect Preparation deck for execution orders, Zenin fallout, and Culling Game setup.
antiLore: Do not reset the cast, institutions, or city consequences to normal immediately after Shibuya.

sourceInfo.title: Shibuya Incident
sourceInfo.notes: Saga draft Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.
